mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold over a 24-month evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in real retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 50% over a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output that supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in over 60% of observations during post-shock recovery periods across a 36-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal robust economic activity that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS changes against 5-day forward returns remains below 0.01 across all rolling 18-month windows in the backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation rate exceeding 0.4% forces expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of months where CPIAUCSL inflation surpasses the 0.4% threshold over a 2-year rolling evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly expansion in real retail sales confirm broad-based demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signal yields a directional accuracy below 52% for 20-day forward returns over a 3-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index during a manufacturing contraction phase signal weakening output that immediately depresses equity prices, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during contraction phases fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns with a win rate above 50% over a 24-month rolling window.
